data.title: Kura Oncology: Compelling Darlifarnib–Cabozantinib Data in Post-IO RCC Support Continued Buy Rating
data.description:
LifeSci Capital analyst Charles Zhu maintained a Buy rating on Kura Oncology today and set a price target of $28.00.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Charles Zhu has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Kura’s emerging clinical data and strategic positioning in renal cell carcinoma. He views the updated results from the FIT-001 study, where darlifarnib is combined with cabozantinib in post-cabo clear cell RCC, as particularly compelling because the response rates materially exceed what would be expected from cabozantinib retreatment alone.

Zhu also highlights that several responses occurred in patients who had previously derived only stable disease or who had recently progressed on cabozantinib, suggesting meaningful resensitization to VEGF TKI therapy. In his view, the manageable safety profile at full-dose cabozantinib, early signals of durability, and evidence of investigator enthusiasm in a high-unmet-need, post-IO setting together support a favorable risk‑reward and justify a continued Buy recommendation on KURA.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Edenred: Cost Tightening and Elevated Net Debt Justify Balanced Hold Recommendation
data.description:
Jefferies analyst Hannes Leitner assigned a Hold rating on EDENRED today and set a price target of €18.20.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Hannes Leitner has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors that balance risks and opportunities for Edenred. While the company is proactively tightening costs through lower headcount and reduced average salaries to offset growing regulatory pressures, this shift underscores that revenue growth is facing more obstacles, limiting near-term upside.

At the same time, underlying net debt remains elevated and constrains financial flexibility, making sizeable acquisitions improbable and capping strategic optionality. With oil-related activities still contributing a meaningful share of operating revenue and most variable compensation targets being met, the business remains fundamentally sound, but not compelling enough on risk‑reward to justify a more positive stance.

In another report released on April 14, Citi also maintained a Hold rating on the stock with a €19.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
